race_brain.main_question: Did the safety cars and rain create or destroy championship opportunities?
race_brain.summary: One of the most chaotic races of the season. Two safety car periods, one VSC, and unexpected rain at lap 57 reshuffled the entire grid. The team that managed tyre transitions fastest gained the most — those who committed to inters early gained 4–6 positions. Verstappen's title lead was reduced but survived due to McLaren's strategic miscalculation on the final SC restart.

true_pace.verdict: Fastest car on pure pace. Compromised by SC timing lap 23 — emerged P4 after pits.
Strong race pace but slower than NOR by 0.25s. Benefited from SC timing lap 57.
Third fastest in clean air. Heavy traffic in mid-stint hurt overall delta vs true pace.

exclusion_log: 3 laps excluded: outliers — rain transition laps
true_pace.verdict: Competitive on HARD but lost time on SOFT in wet-edge conditions. Strategy cost 2 positions.
Slower than NOR by ~0.8s but suffered more traffic. Double-stacked pit hurt clean pace sample.

pit_impact.verdict: SC window — stationary phase optimal but SC neutralised track gap advantage.
Clean stop under SC. Retained P2 with undercut margin preserved.
Slow stop (+2.3s vs NOR). Lost 2 positions — emerged behind HAM and LEC.
Efficient stop. Stayed P5 but missed the undercut window on Norris by 1 lap.
Undercut attempt on lap 20 failed — emerged behind traffic after SC lap 23.

chaos.summary: Extreme race chaos driven by two safety car deployments (laps 23, 58), one VSC (lap 42), unexpected rainfall at lap 57, and three driver investigations. The lap 57 rain period created the single largest position swing of the race — 11 drivers changed position within 3 laps. Peak chaos concentrated laps 55–62.

engineer_notes.title: Safety Car — Lap 23
engineer_notes.message: SC deployed lap 23. 18 drivers affected. Race control message: 'SAFETY CAR DEPLOYED — INCIDENT AT T1'. NOR, VER, HAM within 2 laps of optimal pit window.

engineer_notes.title: HAM slow stop — 2 positions lost
engineer_notes.message: Lap 26 — HAM stop: lane 24.7s. Net position change: -2. Verdict: Slow stop (+2.3s vs NOR). Lost 2 positions — emerged behind HAM and LEC.

engineer_notes.title: NOR undercut attempt blocked by SC
engineer_notes.message: Lap 31 — NOR pits before VER (gap 1.2s). Emerged P4 after pit cycle. SC deployment neutralised track position advantage.

engineer_notes.title: VSC — Lap 42
engineer_notes.message: Lap 42 — VIRTUAL SAFETY CAR deployed. 4 drivers affected. Gave trailing runners opportunity to close gap without racing. LEC closed 3.1s to HAM during VSC.

engineer_notes.title: LEC HARD degradation cliff — lap 29–71
engineer_notes.message: Lap 53 — LEC HARD degradation slope reached +0.082 s/lap. High cliff risk. LEC on 25-lap-old HARD compound with 18 laps remaining — significant drop-off likely.

engineer_notes.title: Rainfall — lap 57 strategy window opens
engineer_notes.message: Lap 57 — Rainfall detected. Track temperature 28°C. Strategy window opens. VER pits immediately for INTERMEDIATE (+0.8s gap to leader converted to +4.2s after 2 laps).

engineer_notes.title: Safety Car — Lap 58 (rain)
engineer_notes.message: Lap 58 — SC deployed due to VAN aquaplaning at T4. Race control: 'SAFETY CAR DEPLOYED — TRACK CONDITIONS'. 14 drivers on wrong compound — forced pit window opens.

engineer_notes.title: NOR pace advantage after restart
engineer_notes.message: Post-restart (lap 61): NOR clean pace 72.691s vs field median 73.8s. McLaren INTERMEDIATE compound performing +1.1s faster than competitors. Track rubber laid over 3 laps eroded advantage.

decisions.title: VER early intermediate gamble — lap 57
decisions.explanation: Verstappen committed to intermediates on lap 57 as the first spots of rain fell. Risk: SC was coming (lap 58) anyway. Reward: emerged P2 after SC restart vs P8 if staying out. Norris waited 1 lap too long and lost the undercut.

decisions.title: NOR missed SC pit window — lap 23
decisions.explanation: McLaren delayed NOR's pit call by 1 lap during the lap 23 SC period. Emerged P4 instead of P1. The track gap advantage of 1.2s was irrelevant under SC — stationary phase penalty compounded by slow traffic exit.

decisions.title: HAM stop slow — 2.3s above reference
decisions.explanation: Hamilton's lap 26 stop took 24.7s lane time vs 22.4s Norris benchmark. The 2.3s excess directly caused emergence behind Leclerc and Piastri. No mechanical issue noted in telemetry — likely wheel nut hesitation.

decisions.title: PIA early stop gamble backfired
decisions.explanation: Piastri pitted lap 20 in a proactive undercut attempt on Verstappen. Emerged P8 due to traffic. The subsequent SC on lap 23 would have been the better window — McLaren's two-car strategy created internal conflict.

decisions.title: LEC VSC window missed — HARD too long
decisions.impact: -2 positions, tyre cliff in final stint
decisions.explanation: Ferrari kept Leclerc out during the lap 42 VSC despite 14-lap-old HARD compound showing 0.071s/lap degradation slope. Emerged with tyres cliff-risking across the final 18 laps. Position lost to Hamilton and Piastri in laps 60–65.
